Gold at Rs 1.53 lakh, silver jumps 5%: Why are prices rising today?The rise in precious metals comes alongside a broader rally in equities and a sharp fall in crude oil prices, as investors adjust to changing risk conditions.

RBI keeps key lending rates unchanged at 5.25%, stance remains 'neutral'The Reserve Bank of India has kept its key policy rate unchanged at 5.25%, marking no change since December 2025, as policymakers chose to wait amid rising global uncertainty linked to the West Asia conflict.

Adani moves US court seeking dismissal of SEC fraud case, says lawsuit lacks jurisdictionIn a pre-motion letter filed ahead of a planned April 30 motion seeking dismissal of the lawsuit, Adanis through their lawyers said the SEC's claims over a 2021 bond sale by the group's renewable energy arm, Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) are legally flawed on multiple grounds.

Lower oil prices ease pressure on inflation and the overall economy, giving the central bank more room to maintain stability.

Sensex jumps 2,800 points: Why is the stock market rising today?As of 9:21 am, the BSE Sensex stood at 77,345.65, up 2,729.07 points or 3.66%, while the Nifty 50 was at 23,918.95, higher by 795.30 points or 3.44%. Investor wealth swelled by over Rs 14 lakh crore in early trade.

The film, set against the 2020 Indo-China conflict, aims to celebrate India and its soldiers without glorifying war.

Badshah pledges women's empowerment song, education for 50 underprivileged girls Rapper Badshah pledged a women's empowerment track within four months amid legal scrutiny over his controversial song, Tateeree. He also promised to sponsor education for 50 underprivileged girls.

I am absolutely heartbroken: Lady Gaga cancels Montreal concert due to illness Lady Gaga cancelled her Montreal show, citing health concerns and expressed a heartfelt apology to fans. The singer said that she was heartbroken over not being able to perform at the concert.

general news.headline: US and Iran agree to 2-week truce, Tehran to open Strait of Hormuz Live UpdatesUS President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran less than two hours before his deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face devastating strikes on its civilian infrastructure.

Countdown to Iran ceasefire: The 90 minutes that stopped a warIt was 6.32 pm. Ninety minutes to the deadline. That was when the post went up, and the war paused, held in place by a 10-point document drafted by Tehran. This is the story of the race to the ceasefire announced by Trump.

Iran, Oman get to levy Hormuz transit fee under 2-week ceasefire plan: ReportThe US-Iran ceasefire plan includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz with a provision allowing Iran and Oman to levy transit fees on ships.

Live: TMC claims poll body chief asked party delegation to 'get lost' during meet over SIRAssembly Election News 2026 Live Updates: Campaigning has ended in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, and the three states have entered the silence period ahead of the April 9 poll day. Meanwhile, protests have erupted in several parts of West Bengal after the 90.66 lakh names were deleted from the electoral rolls. The incident triggered sharp political reactions as Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee took a sharp jibe at the BJP and the Election Commission of India for allegedly targeting people from the Matua and minority communities. She also said that it was her efforts that ensured the inclusion of 32 lakh from 60 lakh voters, whose names were in the adjudication list. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in

Hormuz control, uranium enrichment: Iran's 10-point proposal at centre of US truceIn a statement shortly after Donald Trump's announcement on Tuesday night, Iran's Supreme National Security Council said the US had suspended its "bombing and attack" campaign and accepted the two-week, "double-sided" ceasefire after being compelled by Tehran's military and political pressure.

Eat beef but at home: Himanta Sarma clarifies stand ahead of pollsThe Assam Cattle Preservation Act, 2021, does not prohibit private consumption of beef but bans its sale and consumption in public places, including restaurants, and within a 5-km radius of temples and sattras.

Video: Moon's far side captured in stunning detail during Artemis II fly-byAstronauts aboard Nasa's Artemis II mission captured striking views of the Moon as the Orion spacecraft flew around its far side on April 6, offering a rare glimpse of the rugged, cratered terrain that remains hidden from Earth. The images, taken during the spacecraft's lunar flyby, showcase the stark contrast between the Moon's shadowed far side and the sunlit horizon. The Artemis II mission, Nasa's first crewed journey to the Moon in over five decades, marks a significant milestone in preparations for future lunar landings under the Artemis programme. As Orion completed its trajectory around the Moon, astronauts documented the journey through high-resolution imagery, providing valuable scientific and visual data.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Same CTC, lower take-home? The 50% wage rule explainedYou may still be earning the same CTC, but your in-hand salary could be lower. In this article, we explain how the 50% wage rule is changing salary structures.

Live: Trump says US will help ease Hormuz traffic, calls ceasefire golden age for GulfUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: In a sudden shift from threats to pause, Donald Trump said he would halt US military action against Iran, at least for now, signalling a possible opening for diplomacy. Posting on Truth Social, Trump said, "I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks," framing the move as part of a broader push toward a ceasefire. The decision, he said, followed conversations with Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Army chief Asim Munir, who urged him to hold off on planned strikes. Iran has accepted Pakistan's two-week ceasefire proposal. The ceasefire has been approved by the country's new supreme leader, signalling a possible opening for de-escalation. Iran said talks with the US should not be interpreted as signalling the end of the war, adding that its 10-point proposal calls for lifting all primary and secondary sanctions, full compensation payments, and the release of all frozen Iranian assets. Tehran said it will only accept the war's conclusion once all details are finalised in line with the 10-point plan, Iranian media reported. Negotiations with the US are to be held in Islamabad on 10th April (Friday) to finalise details. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

sports.headline: Bindaas Vaibhav Sooryavanshi forcing Jaiswal to level-up at Rajasthan Royals IPL, RR vs MI: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ripped apart Mumbai Indians in Guwahati on April 7.
